- **Hosts:** Stephan Durry, Torsten Glunde (DDVUG), and Lucas Silbernagel (dbt Labs)

**Event Overview:**
The meetup is described as "networking events open to all folks working with data" focusing on dbt experiences and related topics like analytics engineering and data operations.

**Agenda Timeline:**

events.raw_text:
# Berlin dbt Meetup - August 2023 Event Details

**Event Information:**
- **Date & Time:** Thursday, August 31, 2023, 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM CEST
- **Location:** Ackerstraße 78, Berlin
- **Organizers:** DEPT® & Contentful
- **Venue Host:** Contentful

**Event Description:**
The gathering aims to unite the local Berlin dbt community for networking and cooperation. It welcomes "data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more" who work with data.

**Schedule:**
- 6:00 PM - Welcome with food and beverages
- 6:50 PM - Opening remarks
- 7:00 PM - First presentation
- 7:30 PM - Second presentation
- 8:00 PM - Q&A session

**Featured Speakers:**

1. **Marielle Dado & Eva Schreyer** present "How to Win Friends and Influence People When Migrating to dbt" — exploring challenges of introducing new tools, examining perspectives from both an analytics engineer and data analyst regarding implementation strategies.

2. **Mikkel Dengsøe** (Synq co-founder) discusses "Improving your Data Alerting Workflow" — addressing alert management at scale using dbt metadata features like ownership and importance tracking for faster issue resolution.

**Community Resources:**
Participants are encouraged to join the dbt Slack community and access the #local-berlin channel.

talks.title: How to Win Friends and Influence People When Migrating to dbt
talks.description: Dual perspectives on introducing dbt to organizational tech stacks, discussing migration strategies and overcoming resistance to new tools from both analytics engineer and data analyst viewpoints.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Marielle Dado & Eva Schreyer present "How to Win Friends and Influence People When Migrating to dbt" — exploring challenges of introducing new tools

talks.title: Improving your Data Alerting Workflow
talks.description: Strategies for enhancing data alerting systems using dbt metadata, including issue prioritization, resolution, and debugging efficiency.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Mikkel Dengsøe (Synq co-founder) discusses "Improving your Data Alerting Workflow" — addressing alert management at scale using dbt metadata features

events.raw_text:
# Berlin dbt Meetup - April 2023 Edition

**Event Details:**
- **Date & Time:** Thursday, April 27, 2023, 5:30 PM – 9:00 PM CEST
- **Location:** DEPT® (Hagelberger Str. 53-54, 10965 Berlin)
- **Host:** Sahaya David Durai Jesu R.
- **Organizers:** DEPT® & Contentful

**Event Description:**
The gathering brings together Berlin's data community, welcoming analysts, scientists, engineers, and architects to network and collaborate around dbt technology.

**Schedule:**
- 5:30 PM: Registration (closes at 5:50 PM)
- 6:00 PM: Welcome and networking with refreshments
- 7:00 PM: First presentation
- 7:30 PM: Second presentation
- 8:00 PM: Q&A session and continued networking

**Speakers & Topics:**

Mehdi Ouazza (Data Engineer) presented on combining DuckDB with dbt for "faster testing, reliable data pipelines" and enhanced development workflows.

Zsofia Bognar (Data Insights Lead at Ecosia) discussed managing the company's substantial data volumes—"150-200million events per day"—using dbt alongside storage optimization strategies on Redshift.

**Additional Info:**
Attendees were encouraged to join the dbt Slack community and find the #local-berlin channel for ongoing discussions.

talks.title: Fast and Furious: Unleashing DuckDB & dbt for Analytics Engineers' Local Triumphs
talks.description: Strategies for combining DuckDB with dbt to streamline development workflows and enhance local testing capabilities for analytics engineers.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Mehdi Ouazza (Data Engineer) presented on combining DuckDB with dbt for "faster testing, reliable data pipelines" and enhanced development workflows

talks.title: Massive datasets: Managing our hot-cold storage strategy with dbt at Ecosia
talks.description: Case study on managing 150-200 million daily events at Ecosia using dbt with hot-cold storage optimization strategies on Redshift.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Zsofia Bognar (Data Insights Lead at Ecosia) discussed managing company's substantial data volumes—"150-200million events per day"—using dbt alongside storage optimization strategies on Redshift

# Berlin dbt Meetup - November Edition

**Event Details:**
- **Date & Time:** Wednesday, November 9, 2022, 6:00 PM - 9:00 PM CET
- **Location:** TIER Mobility SE, Eichhornstraße 3, Berlin
- **Host:** Kumar A.

**Agenda:**
- 5:30 PM: Registration opens
- 6:00-7:00 PM: Networking with refreshments
- 6:50-7:00 PM: Welcome remarks from Kumar and Sowmi
- 7:00-7:30 PM: "From Metadata to Metrics" presentation on leveraging dbt Cloud API
- 7:30-8:00 PM: "Minimum Viable Stacks" discussion on data stack architecture for startups
- 8:00-9:00 PM: Q&A and additional networking

**Speakers:**
- **María De Los Ángeles Moreno Corral** (Associate Analytics Engineer, TIER Mobility) and **Jerry Nwabuilo** (Senior Analytics Engineer, TIER Mobility): Discussed "using the dbt Cloud API to track performance and translate metadata into metrics"
- **Saman Arefi** (Data Scientist/Engineer, Project A Ventures): Addressed modern data stack strategies based on analysis of 60+ startups
- **Hosts:** Kumar and Sowmi (Analytics Platform leadership at TIER Mobility)

**Community:** Participants encouraged to join the #local-berlin channel in dbt Slack for discussions.

events.raw_text:
# Berlin dbt Meetup - Event Details

**Event:** Berlin dbt Meetup  
**Date & Time:** Friday, May 6, 2022, 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM CEST  
**Location:** Prater Beer Garden, Kastanienallee 7-9, Berlin, BE  
**Hosts:** dbt L. (Super Organizer) and Tracy R.

## Event Description

This was the third dbt community gathering in Berlin, described as "a casual meetup among data folk, enthusiasts, and practitioners. No talks, just socializing." Attendees could arrive anytime between 6-8 PM at the outdoor venue.

## Target Audience

The meetup welcomed professionals across data roles, including "data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more."

## Community Connection

Organizers encouraged participants to join the dbt Slack community and engage in the dedicated #local-berlin channel for ongoing conversation.

## COVID-19 Policy

The event included a detailed health protocol requiring unvaccinated attendees to participate remotely only, while in-person attendees agreed to follow local health guidelines including masking and social distancing as applicable.

additional_metadata.community_note: Participants encouraged to join dbt Slack #local-berlin channel

- 6:35-6:50 pm: Data exploration and visualization presentation
- 6:50-7:00 pm: Q&A session
- 7:00-7:15 pm: Computational notebooks talk
- 7:15-7:25 pm: Q&A session
- 7:25-7:30 pm: Closing remarks

**Speakers:**

1. **Andras Zimmer** (CTO, Hiflylabs) discussed "Data Exploration and Visualization in the Modern Data Stack," focusing on "when Superset can be an ideal open-source dashboarding companion to dbt"

2. **Jakub Zitny** (Product Engineer, Deepnote) addressed "The Role of Computational Notebooks in the Modern Data Stack," exploring how notebooks integrate with data workflows and ETL processes

3. **Bijan & Ben** (Gemma Analytics co-founders) facilitated as hosts

**Community Connection:**
Participants were encouraged to join the dbt Slack community and access the #local-berlin channel for ongoing conversation during meetups.
